00035 namespace EMF {
00040   const int XMAX_PIXELS = 1024; /*(INT_MAX)*/
00045   const int YMAX_PIXELS = 768; /*(INT_MAX)*/
00051   const int XMAX_MM = 320;
00057   const int YMAX_MM = 240;
00061   const int RESOLUTION = 96;
00065   static inline int ROUND_TO_LONG ( int n ) { return ((n+3)/4)*4; }
00066

00073   struct WCHARSTR {
00074     WCHAR *const string_;       
00075     const int length_;          
00076 
00081     WCHARSTR ( WCHAR *const string, const int length )
00082       : string_( string ), length_( length ) {}
00083   };
00084 
00086 
00091   struct CHARSTR {
00092     CHAR *const string_;        
00093     const int length_;          
00094 
00099     CHARSTR ( CHAR *const string, const int length )
00100       : string_( string ), length_( length ) {}
00101   };
00102 
00104 
00108   struct BYTEARRAY {
00109     BYTE *const array_;         
00110     const int n_;               
00111 
00116     BYTEARRAY ( BYTE *const array, const int n )
00117       : array_( array ), n_( n ) {}
00118   };
00119 
00121 
00124   struct POINTLARRAY {

00126     const DWORD n_;             
00127 
00132     POINTLARRAY ( POINTL *const points, const DWORD n )
00133       : points_( points ), n_( n ) {}

00143

00159

00174     const DWORD n_;             
00175

00188   struct PADDING {
00189     static const char padding_[4]; 
00190     const int size_;            
00191

00199 
00206   class DATASTREAM {
00207     bool swap_;
00208     ::FILE* fp_;
00209 
00210     static bool bigEndian ( void );
00211   public:
00217     DATASTREAM ( ::FILE* fp = 0 ) : swap_( bigEndian() ), fp_( fp ) {}
00222     void setStream ( ::FILE* fp ) { fp_ = fp; }
00227     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const BYTE& byte )
00228     {
00229       fwrite( &byte, sizeof(BYTE), 1, fp_ );
00230       return *this;
00231     }
00236     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( BYTE& byte )
00237     {
00238       fread( &byte, sizeof(BYTE), 1, fp_ );
00239       return *this;
00240     }
00245     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const WORD& word )
00246     {
00247       if ( swap_ ) {
00248         unsigned char const * p = (unsigned char const*)&word;
00249         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00250         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00251       }
00252       else
00253         fwrite( &word, sizeof(WORD), 1, fp_ );
00254       return *this;
00255     }
00260     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( WORD& word )
00261     {
00262       if ( swap_ ) {
00263         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&word;
00264         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00265         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00266       }
00267       else
00268         fread( &word, sizeof(WORD), 1, fp_ );
00269       return *this;
00270     }
00275     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const INT16& word )
00276     {
00277       if ( swap_ ) {
00278         unsigned char const * p = (unsigned char const*)&word;
00279         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00280         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00281       }
00282       else
00283         fwrite( &word, sizeof(INT16), 1, fp_ );
00284       return *this;
00285     }
00290     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( INT16& word )
00291     {
00292       if ( swap_ ) {
00293         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&word;
00294         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00295         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00296       }
00297       else
00298         fread( &word, sizeof(INT16), 1, fp_ );
00299       return *this;
00300     }
00305     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const DWORD& dword )
00306     {
00307       if ( swap_ ) {
00308         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&dword;
00309         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00310         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00311         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00312         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00313       }
00314       else
00315         fwrite( &dword, sizeof(DWORD), 1, fp_ );
00316       return *this;
00317     }
00322     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( DWORD& dword )
00323     {
00324       if ( swap_ ) {
00325         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&dword;
00326         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00327         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00328         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00329         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00330       }
00331       else
00332         fread( &dword, sizeof(DWORD), 1, fp_ );
00333       return *this;
00334     }
00339     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LONG& long_ )
00340     {
00341       if ( swap_ ) {
00342         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&long_;
00343         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00344         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00345         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00346         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00347       }
00348       else
00349         fwrite( &long_, sizeof(LONG), 1, fp_ );
00350       return *this;
00351     }
00356     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LONG& long_ )
00357     {
00358       if ( swap_ ) {
00359         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&long_;
00360         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00361         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00362         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00363         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00364       }
00365       else
00366         fread( &long_, sizeof(LONG), 1, fp_ );
00367       return *this;
00368     }
00373     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const INT& int_ )
00374     {
00375       if ( swap_ ) {
00376         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&int_;
00377         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00378         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00379         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00380         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00381       }
00382       else
00383         fwrite( &int_, sizeof(INT), 1, fp_ );
00384       return *this;
00385     }
00390     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( INT& int_ )
00391     {
00392       if ( swap_ ) {
00393         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&int_;
00394         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00395         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00396         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00397         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00398       }
00399       else
00400         fread( &int_, sizeof(INT), 1, fp_ );
00401       return *this;
00402     }
00407     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const UINT& uint )
00408     {
00409       if ( swap_ ) {
00410         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&uint;
00411         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00412         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00413         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00414         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00415       }
00416       else
00417         fwrite( &uint, sizeof(UINT), 1, fp_ );
00418       return *this;
00419     }
00424     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( UINT& uint )
00425     {
00426       if ( swap_ ) {
00427         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&uint;
00428         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00429         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00430         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00431         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00432       }
00433       else
00434         fread( &uint, sizeof(UINT), 1, fp_ );
00435       return *this;
00436     }
00441     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const FLOAT& float_ )
00442     {
00443       if ( swap_ ) {
00444         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&float_;
00445         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00446         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00447         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00448         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00449       }
00450       else
00451         fwrite( &float_, sizeof(FLOAT), 1, fp_ );
00452       return *this;
00453     }
00458     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( FLOAT& float_ )
00459     {
00460       if ( swap_ ) {
00461         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&float_;
00462         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00463         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00464         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00465         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00466       }
00467       else
00468         fread( &float_, sizeof(FLOAT), 1, fp_ );
00469       return *this;
00470     }
00475     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const PADDING& padding )
00476     {
00477       if ( padding.size_ != 0 )
00478         fwrite( &padding.padding_, sizeof(CHAR), padding.size_, fp_ );
00479       return *this;
00480     }
00485     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const RECTL& rectl )
00486     {
00487       *this << rectl.left << rectl.top << rectl.right << rectl.bottom;
00488       return *this;
00489     }
00494     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( RECTL& rectl )
00495     {
00496       *this >> rectl.left >> rectl.top >> rectl.right >> rectl.bottom;
00497       return *this;
00498     }
00503     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const SIZEL& sizel )
00504     {
00505       *this << sizel.cx << sizel.cy;
00506       return *this;
00507     }
00512     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( SIZEL& sizel )
00513     {
00514       *this >> sizel.cx >> sizel.cy;
00515       return *this;
00516     }
00521     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const WCHARSTR& wcharstr )
00522     {
00523       for ( int i = 0; i < wcharstr.length_; i++ )
00524         *this << wcharstr.string_[i];
00525       return *this;
00526     }
00531     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( WCHARSTR& wcharstr )
00532     {
00533       for ( int i = 0; i < wcharstr.length_; i++ )
00534         *this >> wcharstr.string_[i];
00535       return *this;
00536     }
00541     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const CHARSTR& charstr )
00542     {
00543       fwrite( charstr.string_, sizeof(CHAR), charstr.length_, fp_ );
00544       return *this;
00545     }
00550     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( CHARSTR& charstr )
00551     {
00552       fread( charstr.string_, sizeof(CHAR), charstr.length_, fp_ );
00553       return *this;
00554     }
00559     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const ::EMR& emr )
00560     {
00561       *this << emr.iType << emr.nSize;
00562       return *this;
00563     }
00568     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( ::EMR& emr )
00569     {
00570       *this >> emr.iType >> emr.nSize;
00571       return *this;
00572     }
00577     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINT& point )
00578     {
00579       *this << point.x << point.y;
00580       return *this;
00581     }
00586     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINT& point )
00587     {
00588       *this >> point.x >> point.y;
00589       return *this;
00590     }
00595     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINTL& pointl )
00596     {
00597       *this << pointl.x << pointl.y;
00598       return *this;
00599     }
00604     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINTL& pointl )
00605     {
00606       *this >> pointl.x >> pointl.y;
00607       return *this;
00608     }
00613     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINT16& point )
00614     {
00615       *this << point.x << point.y;
00616       return *this;
00617     }
00622     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINT16& point )
00623     {
00624       *this >> point.x >> point.y;
00625       return *this;
00626     }
00631     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const XFORM& xform )
00632     {
00633       *this << xform.eM11 << xform.eM12 << xform.eM21 << xform.eM22
00634             << xform.eDx << xform.eDy;
00635       return *this;
00636     }
00641     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( XFORM& xform )
00642     {
00643       *this >> xform.eM11 >> xform.eM12 >> xform.eM21 >> xform.eM22
00644             >> xform.eDx >> xform.eDy;
00645       return *this;
00646     }
00651     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const BYTEARRAY& array )
00652     {
00653       fwrite( array.array_, sizeof(BYTE), array.n_, fp_ );
00654       return *this;
00655     }
00660     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( BYTEARRAY& array )
00661     {
00662       fread( array.array_, sizeof(BYTE), array.n_, fp_ );
00663       return *this;
00664     }
00669     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINTLARRAY& array )
00670     {
00671       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00672         *this << array.points_[i];
00673       return *this;
00674     }
00679     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINTLARRAY& array )
00680     {
00681       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00682         *this >> array.points_[i];
00683       return *this;
00684     }
00689     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const POINT16ARRAY& array )
00690     {
00691       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00692         *this << array.points_[i];
00693       return *this;
00694     }
00699     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( POINT16ARRAY& array )
00700     {
00701       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00702         *this >> array.points_[i];
00703       return *this;
00704     }
00709     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const INTARRAY& array )
00710     {
00711       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00712         *this << array.ints_[i];
00713       return *this;
00714     }
00719     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( INTARRAY& array )
00720     {
00721       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00722         *this >> array.ints_[i];
00723       return *this;
00724     }
00729     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const DWORDARRAY& array )
00730     {
00731       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00732         *this << array.dwords_[i];
00733       return *this;
00734     }
00739     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( DWORDARRAY& array )
00740     {
00741       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < array.n_; i++ )
00742         *this >> array.dwords_[i];
00743       return *this;
00744     }
00749     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const ::EMRTEXT& text )
00750     {
00751       *this << text.ptlReference << text.nChars << text.offString << text.fOptions
00752          << text.rcl << text.offDx;
00753       return *this;
00754     }
00759     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( ::EMRTEXT& text )
00760     {
00761       *this >> text.ptlReference >> text.nChars >> text.offString >> text.fOptions
00762             >> text.rcl >> text.offDx;
00763       return *this;
00764     }
00769     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGPEN& pen )
00770     {
00771       *this << pen.lopnStyle << pen.lopnWidth << pen.lopnColor;
00772       return *this;
00773     }
00778     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGPEN& pen )
00779     {
00780       *this >> pen.lopnStyle >> pen.lopnWidth >> pen.lopnColor;
00781       return *this;
00782     }
00787     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const EXTLOGPEN& pen )
00788     {
00789       // *** How big is this structure if there are no style entries? ***
00790       *this << pen.elpPenStyle << pen.elpWidth << pen.elpBrushStyle << pen.elpColor
00791             << pen.elpHatch << pen.elpNumEntries;
00792       return *this;
00793     }
00798     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( EXTLOGPEN& pen )
00799     {
00800       // *** How big is this structure if there are no style entries? ***
00801       *this >> pen.elpPenStyle >> pen.elpWidth >> pen.elpBrushStyle >> pen.elpColor
00802             >> pen.elpHatch >> pen.elpNumEntries;
00803       return *this;
00804     }
00809     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGBRUSH& brush )
00810     {
00811       *this << brush.lbStyle << brush.lbColor << brush.lbHatch;
00812       return *this;
00813     }
00818     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGBRUSH& brush )
00819     {
00820       *this >> brush.lbStyle >> brush.lbColor >> brush.lbHatch;
00821       return *this;
00822     }
00827     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGFONTW& font )
00828     {
00829       *this << font.lfHeight << font.lfWidth << font.lfEscapement
00830             << font.lfOrientation << font.lfWeight << font.lfItalic
00831             << font.lfUnderline << font.lfStrikeOut << font.lfCharSet
00832             << font.lfOutPrecision << font.lfClipPrecision << font.lfQuality
00833             << font.lfPitchAndFamily
00834             << WCHARSTR( const_cast<WCHAR*const>(font.lfFaceName), LF_FACESIZE );
00835       return *this;
00836     }
00841     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGFONTW& font )
00842     {
00843       WCHARSTR wFaceName( font.lfFaceName, LF_FACESIZE );
00844 
00845       *this >> font.lfHeight >> font.lfWidth >> font.lfEscapement
00846             >> font.lfOrientation >> font.lfWeight >> font.lfItalic
00847             >> font.lfUnderline >> font.lfStrikeOut >> font.lfCharSet
00848             >> font.lfOutPrecision >> font.lfClipPrecision >> font.lfQuality
00849             >> font.lfPitchAndFamily
00850             >> wFaceName;
00851       return *this;
00852     }
00857     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const PANOSE& panose )
00858     {
00859       fwrite( &panose, sizeof(PANOSE), 1, fp_ );
00860       return *this;
00861     }
00866     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( PANOSE& panose )
00867     {
00868       fread( &panose, sizeof(PANOSE), 1, fp_ );
00869       return *this;
00870     }
00875     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const EXTLOGFONTW& font )
00876     {
00877       *this << font.elfLogFont
00878             << WCHARSTR( const_cast<WCHAR*const>(font.elfFullName),
00879                          LF_FULLFACESIZE )
00880             << WCHARSTR( const_cast<WCHAR*const>(font.elfStyle), LF_FACESIZE )
00881             << font.elfVersion << font.elfStyleSize << font.elfMatch
00882             << font.elfReserved
00883             << BYTEARRAY( const_cast<BYTE*const>(font.elfVendorId),
00884                           ELF_VENDOR_SIZE )
00885             << font.elfCulture << font.elfPanose;
00886       return *this;
00887     }
00892     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( EXTLOGFONTW& font )
00893     {
00894       WCHARSTR wFullName( font.elfFullName, LF_FULLFACESIZE );
00895       WCHARSTR wStyle( font.elfStyle, LF_FACESIZE );
00896       BYTEARRAY bVendorId( font.elfVendorId, ELF_VENDOR_SIZE );
00897       *this >> font.elfLogFont
00898             >> wFullName >> wStyle
00899             >> font.elfVersion >> font.elfStyleSize >> font.elfMatch
00900             >> font.elfReserved >> bVendorId
00901             >> font.elfCulture >> font.elfPanose;
00902       return *this;
00903     }
00908     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGPALETTE& palette )
00909     {
00910       // *** How big is this structure if the palette is empty? ***
00911       *this << palette.palVersion << palette.palNumEntries;
00912       return *this;
00913     }
00918     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGPALETTE& palette )
00919     {
00920       // *** How big is this structure if the palette is empty? ***
00921       *this >> palette.palVersion >> palette.palNumEntries;
00922       return *this;
00923     }
00924   };
00925 
00926   class METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT;
00927 
00929 
00935   class METARECORD {
00936   public:
00943     virtual void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const = 0;
00950     virtual bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds ) = 0;
00956     virtual int size ( void ) const = 0;
00962     virtual ~METARECORD( ) { }
00963 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
00964 
00968     virtual void edit ( void ) const {}
00969 #endif
00970   };
00971 
00972 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
00973   /* Miscellaneous editing routines */
00974   inline void edit_rectl ( const char* tag, const RECTL& rectl )
00975   {
00976     printf( "\t%s\t: (%ld, %ld) - (%ld, %ld)\n", tag, rectl.left, rectl.top,
00977             rectl.right, rectl.bottom );
00978   }
00979 
00980   inline void edit_pen_style ( const char* tag, DWORD style )
00981   {
00982     printf( "\t%s : ", tag );
00983     switch ( style & PS_STYLE_MASK ) {
00984     case PS_SOLID: printf( "PS_SOLID" ); break;
00985     case PS_DASH: printf( "PS_DASH" ); break;
00986     case PS_DOT: printf( "PS_DOT" ); break;
00987     case PS_DASHDOT: printf( "PS_DASHDOT" ); break;
00988     case PS_DASHDOTDOT: printf( "PS_DASHDOTDOT" ); break;
00989     case PS_NULL: printf( "PS_NULL" ); break;
00990     case PS_INSIDEFRAME: printf( "PS_INSIDEFRAME" ); break;
00991     case PS_USERSTYLE: printf( "PS_USERSTYLE" ); break;
00992     case PS_ALTERNATE: printf( "PS_ALTERNATE" ); break;
00993     }
00994     switch ( style & PS_ENDCAP_MASK ) {
00995     case PS_ENDCAP_ROUND: printf( " | PS_ENDCAP_ROUND" ); break;
00996     case PS_ENDCAP_SQUARE: printf( " | PS_ENDCAP_SQUARE" ); break;
00997     case PS_ENDCAP_FLAT: printf( " | PS_ENDCAP_FLAT" ); break;
00998     }
00999     switch ( style & PS_JOIN_MASK ) {
01000     case PS_JOIN_ROUND: printf( " | PS_JOIN_ROUND" ); break;
01001     case PS_JOIN_BEVEL: printf( " | PS_JOIN_BEVEL" ); break;
01002     case PS_JOIN_MITER: printf( " | PS_JOIN_MITER" ); break;
01003     }
01004     switch ( style & PS_TYPE_MASK ) {
01005     case PS_COSMETIC: printf( " | PS_COSMETIC" ); break;
01006     case PS_GEOMETRIC: printf( " | PS_GEOMETRIC" ); break;
01007     }
01008     printf( "\n" );
01009   }
01010 
01011   inline void edit_brush_style ( const char* tag, DWORD style )
01012   {
01013     printf( "\t%s : ", tag );
01014     switch ( style ) {
01015     case BS_SOLID: printf( "BS_SOLID" ); break;
01016     case BS_NULL: printf( "BS_NULL" ); break;
01017     case BS_HATCHED: printf( "BS_HATCHED" ); break;
01018     case BS_PATTERN: printf( "BS_PATTERN" ); break;
01019     case BS_INDEXED: printf( "BS_INDEXED" ); break;
01020     case BS_DIBPATTERN: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERN" ); break;
01021     case BS_DIBPATTERNPT: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERNPT" ); break;
01022     case BS_PATTERN8X8: printf( "BS_PATTERN8X8" ); break;
01023     case BS_DIBPATTERN8X8: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERN8X8" ); break;
01024     case BS_MONOPATTERN: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERN8X8" ); break;
01025     default: printf( "unknown(%ld)", style );
01026     }
01027     printf( "\n" );
01028   }
01029 
01030   inline void edit_brush_hatch ( const char* tag, DWORD hatch )
01031   {
01032     printf( "\t%s : ", tag );
01033     switch ( hatch ) {
01034     case HS_HORIZONTAL: printf( "HS_HORIZONTAL" ); break;
01035     case HS_VERTICAL: printf( "HS_VERTICAL" ); break;
01036     case HS_FDIAGONAL: printf( "HS_FDIAGONAL" ); break;
01037     case HS_BDIAGONAL: printf( "HS_BDIAGONAL" ); break;
01038     case HS_CROSS: printf( "HS_CROSS" ); break;
01039     case HS_DIAGCROSS: printf( "HS_DIAGCROSS" ); break;
01040     default: printf( "unknown(%ld)", hatch );
01041     }
01042     printf( "\n" );
01043   }
01044 #endif
01045 
01052   enum OBJECTTYPE { O_METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT     = OBJ_METADC,
01053                     O_FONT                      = OBJ_FONT,
01054                     O_PEN                       = OBJ_PEN,
01055                     O_EXTPEN                    = OBJ_EXTPEN,
01056                     O_BRUSH                     = OBJ_BRUSH,
01057                     O_PALETTE                   = OBJ_PAL };
01058 #if 0
01059 
01062   static char* typStr ( OBJECTTYPE type )
01063   {
01064     switch (type) {
01065     case O_METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT:
01066       return "metafile device context";
01067     case O_FONT:
01068       return "font";
01069     case O_PEN:
01070       return "pen";
01071     case O_EXTPEN:
01072       return "extended pen";
01073     case O_BRUSH:
01074       return "brush";
01075     case O_PALETTE:
01076       return "palette";
01077     }
01078     return "unknown object";
01079   }
01080 #endif
01081 
01082 
01087   class OBJECT {
01088   public:
01089     HGDIOBJ handle;             
01090 
01091     virtual ~OBJECT () {}
01096     OBJECT ( void ) : handle( 0 ) {}
01100     virtual OBJECTTYPE getType ( void ) const = 0;
01101   };
01102 
01104 
01109   class GRAPHICSOBJECT : public OBJECT {
01110   public:
01112     virtual ~GRAPHICSOBJECT () {}
01117     std::map< HDC, HGDIOBJ > contexts;
01123     virtual METARECORD* newEMR ( HDC dc, HGDIOBJ handle ) = 0;
01124   };
01125 
01126   typedef METARECORD*(*METARECORDCTOR)(DATASTREAM&);
01127 
01128   class GLOBALOBJECTS {
01132     std::vector<OBJECT*> objects;
01133 
01140     std::map< DWORD, METARECORDCTOR > new_records;
01141 
01142   public:
01143     GLOBALOBJECTS ( void );
01144     ~GLOBALOBJECTS ( void );
01145     HGDIOBJ add ( OBJECT* object );
01146     OBJECT* find ( const HGDIOBJ handle );
01147     void remove ( const OBJECT* object );
01148 
01149     std::vector<EMF::OBJECT*>::const_iterator begin ( void ) const
01150     { return objects.begin(); }
01151     std::vector<EMF::OBJECT*>::const_iterator end ( void ) const
01152     { return objects.end(); }
01153 
01154     METARECORDCTOR newRecord ( DWORD iType ) const;
01155 
01156     static EMF::METARECORD* new_eof ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01157     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setviewportorgex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01158     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setwindoworgex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01159     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setviewportextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01160     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setwindowextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01161     static EMF::METARECORD* new_scaleviewportextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01162     static EMF::METARECORD* new_scalewindowextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01163     static EMF::METARECORD* new_modifyworldtransform ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01164     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setworldtransform ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01165     static EMF::METARECORD* new_settextalign ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01166     static EMF::METARECORD* new_settextcolor ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01167     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setbkcolor ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01168     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setbkmode ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01169     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setpolyfillmode ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01170     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setmapmode ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01171     static EMF::METARECORD* new_selectobject ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01172     static EMF::METARECORD* new_deleteobject ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01173     static EMF::METARECORD* new_movetoex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01174     static EMF::METARECORD* new_lineto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01175     static EMF::METARECORD* new_arc ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01176     static EMF::METARECORD* new_arcto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01177     static EMF::METARECORD* new_rectangle ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01178     static EMF::METARECORD* new_ellipse ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01179     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polyline ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01180     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polyline16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01181     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polygon ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01182     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polygon16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01183     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polypolygon ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01184     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polypolygon16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01185     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezier ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01186     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezier16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01187     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezierto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01188     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezierto16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01189     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polylineto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01190     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polylineto16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01191     static EMF::METARECORD* new_exttextouta ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01192     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setpixelv ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01193     static EMF::METARECORD* new_createpen ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01194     static EMF::METARECORD* new_extcreatepen ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01195     static EMF::METARECORD* new_createbrushindirect ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01196     static EMF::METARECORD* new_extcreatefontindirectw ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01197     static EMF::METARECORD* new_fillpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01198     static EMF::METARECORD* new_strokepath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01199     static EMF::METARECORD* new_beginpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01200     static EMF::METARECORD* new_endpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01201     static EMF::METARECORD* new_closefigure ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01202     static EMF::METARECORD* new_savedc ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01203     static EMF::METARECORD* new_restoredc ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01204     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setmetargn ( DATASTREAM& ds );
01205   };

01210 
01216   class ENHMETAHEADER : public METARECORD, public ::ENHMETAHEADER {
01217 
01218     LPWSTR description_w;
01219     int description_size;
01220 
01221   public:
01228     ENHMETAHEADER ( LPCWSTR description = 0 )
01229       : description_w( 0 ), description_size( 0 )
01230     {
01231       iType = EMR_HEADER;
01232       nSize = sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER );
01233 
01234       // Compute the bounds
01235       RECTL default_bounds = { 0, 0, 0, 0 };
01236       rclBounds = default_bounds;
01237       RECTL default_frame = { 0, 0, 0, 0 };
01238       rclFrame = default_frame;
01239       dSignature = ENHMETA_SIGNATURE;
01240       nVersion = 0x10000;
01241       nBytes = nSize;
01242       nRecords = 1;
01243       nHandles = 0;
01244       sReserved = 0;
01245       nDescription = 0;
01246       offDescription = 0;
01247       nPalEntries = 0;
01248       szlDevice.cx = XMAX_PIXELS;
01249       szlDevice.cy = YMAX_PIXELS;
01250       szlMillimeters.cx = XMAX_MM;
01251       szlMillimeters.cy = YMAX_MM;
01252       //
01253       cbPixelFormat = 0;
01254       offPixelFormat = 0;
01255       bOpenGL = FALSE;
01256       //
01257 #if 0
01258       szlMicrometers.cx = 1000 * szlMillimeters.cx;
01259       szlMicrometers.cy = 1000 * szlMillimeters.cy;
01260 #endif
01261       if ( description ) {
01262         // Count the number of characters in the description
01263         int description_count = 0, nulls = 0;
01264         LPCWSTR description_p = description;
01265         while ( nulls < 3 ) {
01266           description_count++;
01267           if ( (*description_p++) == 0 ) nulls++;
01268         }
01269 
01270         // Make sure that the TOTAL record length will be a multiple of 4
01271 
01272         int record_size = ROUND_TO_LONG( sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER ) +
01273                                          sizeof( WCHAR ) * description_count );
01274         description_size =
01275           (record_size - sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER )) / sizeof( WCHAR );
01276 
01277         description_w = new WCHAR[ description_size ];
01278 
01279         memset( description_w, 0, sizeof(WCHAR) * description_size );
01280 
01281         for ( int i=0; i<description_count; i++ )
01282           description_w[i] = *description++;
01283 
01284         nSize = nBytes = record_size;
01285         nDescription = description_count;
01286         offDescription = sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER );
01287       }
01288     }
01289     
01293     ~ENHMETAHEADER ( )
01294     {
01295       if ( description_w ) delete[] description_w;
01296     }
01301     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01302     {
01303       ds << iType << nSize
01304          << rclBounds << rclFrame
01305          << dSignature << nVersion << nBytes << nRecords << nHandles << sReserved
01306          << nDescription << offDescription << nPalEntries
01307          << szlDevice << szlMillimeters
01308          << cbPixelFormat << offPixelFormat << bOpenGL
01309 #if 0
01310          << szlMicrometers
01311 #endif
01312          << WCHARSTR( description_w, description_size );
01313       return true;
01314     }
01318     bool unserialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01319     {
01320       ds >> iType >> nSize
01321          >> rclBounds >> rclFrame
01322          >> dSignature >> nVersion >> nBytes >> nRecords >> nHandles >> sReserved
01323          >> nDescription >> offDescription >> nPalEntries
01324          >> szlDevice >> szlMillimeters;
01325 
01326       // Some elements of the metafile header were added at later dates
01327 
01328 #define OffsetOf( a, b ) ((unsigned int)(((char*)&(((::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)->b)) - \
01329 (char*)((::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)))
01330 
01331 #if 0
01332       if ( OffsetOf( this, szlMicrometers ) <= offDescription )
01333         ds >> cbPixelFormat >> offPixelFormat >> bOpenGL;
01334 #else
01335       if ( sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER ) <= offDescription )
01336         ds >> cbPixelFormat >> offPixelFormat >> bOpenGL;
01337 #endif
01338 #undef OffsetOf
01339 #if 0
01340       if ( sizeof(::ENHMETAHEADER) <= offDescription )
01341         ds >> szlMicrometers;
01342 #endif
01343       // Should now probably check that the offset is correct...
01344 
01345       description_size = ( nSize - offDescription ) / sizeof(WCHAR);
01346       description_w = new WCHAR[ description_size ];
01347 
01348       WCHARSTR description( description_w, description_size );
01349 
01350       ds >> description;
01351 
01352       return true;
01353     }
01357     int size ( void ) const { return nSize; }
01363     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC /*dc*/ ) const
01364     {
01365       // Actually handled by the destination device context.
01366     }
01367 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01368 
01371     void edit ( void ) const
01372     {
01373       printf( "*HEADER*\n" );
01374       printf( "\tiType             : %ld\n", iType );
01375       printf( "\tnSize             : %ld\n", nSize );
01376 #if 0
01377       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
01378       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
01379       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
01380       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
01381 #else
01382       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
01383 #endif
01384 #if 0
01385       printf( "\trclFrame.left     : %ld\n", rclFrame.left );
01386       printf( "\trclFrame.top      : %ld\n", rclFrame.top );
01387       printf( "\trclFrame.right    : %ld\n", rclFrame.right );
01388       printf( "\trclFrame.bottom   : %ld\n", rclFrame.bottom );
01389 #else
01390       edit_rectl( "rclFrame", rclFrame );
01391 #endif
01392       printf( "\tdSignature        : %.4s\n", (const char*)&dSignature );
01393       printf( "\tnVersion          : 0x%x\n", (unsigned int)nVersion );
01394       printf( "\tnBytes            : %ld\n", nBytes );
01395       printf( "\tnRecords          : %ld\n", nRecords );
01396       printf( "\tnHandles          : %d\n", nHandles );
01397       printf( "\tnDescription      : %ld\n", nDescription );
01398       printf( "\toffDescription    : %ld\n", offDescription );
01399       printf( "\tnPalEntries       : %ld\n", nPalEntries );
01400       printf( "\tszlDevice.cx      : %ld\n", szlDevice.cx );
01401       printf( "\tszlDevice.cy      : %ld\n", szlDevice.cy );
01402       printf( "\tszlMillimeters.cx : %ld\n", szlMillimeters.cx );
01403       printf( "\tszlMillimeters.cy : %ld\n", szlMillimeters.cy );
01404 
01405       /* Make a crude guess as to the age of this file */
01406 #define OffsetOf( a, b ) ((unsigned int)(((const char*)&(((const ::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)->b)) - \
01407 (const char*)((const ::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)))
01408 
01409       if ( OffsetOf( this, cbPixelFormat ) <= offDescription ) {
01410         printf( "\tcbPixelFormat     : %ld\n", cbPixelFormat );
01411         printf( "\toffPixelFormat    : %ld\n", offPixelFormat );
01412         printf( "\tbOpenGL           : %ld\n", bOpenGL );
01413 #if 0
01414         if ( sizeof(::ENHMETAHEADER) <= offDescription ) {
01415           printf( "\tszlMicrometers.cx : %ld\n", szlMicrometers.cx );
01416           printf( "\tszlMicrometers.cy : %ld\n", szlMicrometers.cy );
01417         }
01418 #endif
01419       }
01420 
01421 #undef OffsetOf
01422 
01423       if ( nDescription != 0 ) {
01424 
01425         wchar_t last_w = 0;
01426         WCHAR* description = description_w;
01427 
01428         printf( "\tDescription:" );
01429 
01430         for ( DWORD i = 0; i < nDescription; i++ ) {
01431 
01432           wchar_t w = *description++; /* This is not true, really. UNICODE is not
01433                                        * glibc's wide character representation */
01434 
01435           if ( w != 0 ) {
01436             if ( last_w == 0 ) printf( "\n\t\t" );
01437             putchar( w );
01438           }
01439           
01440           last_w = w;
01441         }
01442         printf( "\n" );
01443       }
01444     }
01445 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01446   };
01447 
01449 
01454   class EMREOF : public METARECORD, ::EMREOF {
01455   public:
01459     EMREOF ( void )
01460     {
01461       emr.iType = EMR_EOF;
01462       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMREOF );
01463       nPalEntries = 0;
01464       offPalEntries = 0;
01465       nSizeLast = 0;
01466     }
01467 
01472     EMREOF ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01473     {
01474       ds >> emr >> nPalEntries >> offPalEntries >> nSizeLast;
01475     }
01476 
01480     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01481     {
01482       ds << emr << nPalEntries << offPalEntries << nSizeLast;
01483       return true;
01484     }
01488     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01494     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC /*dc*/ ) const
01495     {
01496       // Actually handled by the destination device context.
01497     }
01498 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01499 
01502     void edit ( void ) const
01503     {
01504       printf( "*EOF*\n" );
01505     }
01506 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01507   };
01508

01515   class EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX : public METARECORD, ::EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX {
01516   public:
01521     EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX ( INT x, INT y )
01522     {
01523       emr.iType = EMR_SETVIEWPORTORGEX;
01524       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX );
01525       ptlOrigin.x = x;
01526       ptlOrigin.y = y;
01527     }
01532     EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01533     {
01534       ds >> emr >> ptlOrigin;
01535     }
01539     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01540     {
01541       ds << emr << ptlOrigin;
01542       return true;
01543     }
01547     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01553     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
01554     {
01555       SetViewportOrgEx( dc, ptlOrigin.x, ptlOrigin.y, 0 );
01556     }
01557 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01558 
01561     void edit ( void ) const
01562     {
01563       printf( "*SETVIEWPORTORGEX*\n" );
01564       printf( "\tptlOrigin: %ld, %ld\n", ptlOrigin.x, ptlOrigin.y );
01565     }
01566 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01567   };

01697   class EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX : public METARECORD, ::EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX {
01698   public:
01705     EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX ( LONG x_num, LONG x_den, LONG y_num, LONG y_den )
01706     {
01707       emr.iType = EMR_SCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX;
01708       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX );
01709       xNum = x_num;
01710       xDenom = x_den;
01711       yNum = y_num;
01712       yDenom = y_den;
01713     }
01718     EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01719     {
01720       ds >> emr >> xNum >> xDenom >> yNum >> yDenom;
01721     }
01725     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01726     {
01727       ds << emr << xNum << xDenom << yNum << yDenom;
01728       return true;
01729     }
01733     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01739     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
01740     {
01741       ScaleViewportExtEx( dc, xNum, xDenom, yNum, yDenom, 0 );
01742     }
01743 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01744 
01747     void edit ( void ) const
01748     {
01749       printf( "*SCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX*\n" );
01750       printf( "\txNum: %ld\n", xNum );
01751       printf( "\txDenom: %ld\n", xDenom );
01752       printf( "\tyNum: %ld\n", yNum );
01753       printf( "\tyDenom: %ld\n", yDenom );
01754     }
01755 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01756   };
01757 
01759 
01764   class EMRSETWINDOWEXTEX : public METARECORD, ::EMRSETWINDOWEXTEX {
01765   public:
01770     EMRSETWINDOWEXTEX ( INT cx, INT cy )
01771     {
01772       emr.iType = EMR_SETWINDOWEXTEX;
01773       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRSETWINDOWEXTEX );
01774       szlExtent.cx = cx;
01775       szlExtent.cy = cy;
01776     }
01781     EMRSETWINDOWEXTEX ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01782     {
01783       ds >> emr >> szlExtent;
01784     }
01788     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01789     {
01790       ds << emr << szlExtent;
01791       return true;
01792     }
01796     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01802     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
01803     {
01804       SetWindowExtEx( dc, szlExtent.cx, szlExtent.cy, 0 );
01805     }
01806 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01807 
01810     void edit ( void ) const
01811     {
01812       printf( "*SETWINDOWEXTEX*\n" );
01813       printf( "\tszlExtent: %ld, %ld\n", szlExtent.cx, szlExtent.cy );
01814     }
01815 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01816   };

01892   class EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM : public METARECORD, ::EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM {
01893   public:
01899     EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM ( const XFORM* transform, DWORD mode )
01900     {
01901       emr.iType = EMR_MODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM;
01902       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM );
01903       xform = *transform;
01904       iMode = mode;
01905     }
01910     EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01911     {
01912       ds >> emr >> xform >> iMode;
01913     }
01917     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01918     {
01919       ds << emr << xform << iMode;
01920       return true;
01921     }
01925     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01931     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
01932     {
01933       ModifyWorldTransform( dc, &xform, iMode );
01934     }
01935 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01936 
01939     void edit ( void ) const
01940     {
01941       printf( "*MODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM*\n" );
01942       printf( "\txform.eM11  : %f\n", xform.eM11 );
01943       printf( "\txform.eM12  : %f\n", xform.eM12 );
01944       printf( "\txform.eM21  : %f\n", xform.eM21 );
01945       printf( "\txform.eM22  : %f\n", xform.eM22 );
01946       printf( "\txform.eDx   : %f\n", xform.eDx );
01947       printf( "\txform.eDy   : %f\n", xform.eDy );
01948       printf( "\tiMode: " );
01949       switch ( iMode ) {
01950       case MWT_IDENTITY: printf( "MWT_IDENTITY\n" ); break;
01951       case MWT_LEFTMULTIPLY: printf( "MWT_LEFTMULTIPLY\n" ); break;
01952       case MWT_RIGHTMULTIPLY: printf( "MWT_RIGHTMULTIPLY\n" ); break;
01953       default: printf( "unknown(%ld)\n", iMode );
01954       }
01955     }
01956 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01957   };
01958

02015       printf( "\txform.eM21  : %f\n", xform.eM21 );

02017       printf( "\txform.eDx   : %f\n", xform.eDx );

02027   class EMRSETTEXTALIGN : public METARECORD, ::EMRSETTEXTALIGN {
02028   public:
02032     EMRSETTEXTALIGN ( UINT mode )
02033     {
02034       emr.iType = EMR_SETTEXTALIGN;
02035       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRSETTEXTALIGN );
02036       iMode = mode;
02037     }
02042     EMRSETTEXTALIGN ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02043     {
02044       ds >> emr >> iMode;
02045     }
02049     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02050     {
02051       ds << emr << iMode;
02052       return true;
02053     }
02057     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02063     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
02064     {
02065       SetTextAlign( dc, iMode );
02066     }
02067 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02068 
02071     void edit ( void ) const
02072     {
02073       unsigned int known_bits = TA_BASELINE+TA_CENTER+TA_UPDATECP+TA_RTLREADING;
02074       unsigned int unknown_bits = ~known_bits;
02075 
02076       printf( "*SETTEXTALIGN*\n" );
02077       printf( "\tiMode: " );
02078       if ( iMode & TA_UPDATECP )
02079         printf( "TA_UPDATECP" );
02080       else
02081         printf( "TA_NOUPDATECP" );
02082       if ( iMode & TA_CENTER )
02083         printf( " | TA_CENTER" );
02084       else if ( iMode & TA_RIGHT )
02085         printf( " | TA_RIGHT" );
02086       else
02087         printf( " | TA_LEFT" );
02088       if ( iMode & TA_BASELINE )
02089         printf( " | TA_BASELINE" );
02090       else if ( iMode & TA_BOTTOM )
02091         printf( " | TA_BOTTOM" );
02092       else
02093         printf( " | TA_TOP" );
02094       if ( iMode & TA_RTLREADING )
02095         printf( " | TA_RTLREADING" );
02096       if ( iMode & unknown_bits )
02097         printf( " | unknown bits(0x%lx)", iMode & unknown_bits );
02098       printf( "\n" );
02099     }
02100 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02101   };
02102 
02104

02632   class EMRARC : public METARECORD, ::EMRARC {
02633   public:
02645     EMRARC ( INT left, INT top, INT right, INT bottom, INT xstart,
02646              INT ystart, INT xend, INT yend )
02647     {
02648       emr.iType = EMR_ARC;
02649       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRARC );
02650       rclBox.left = left;
02651       rclBox.right = right;
02652       rclBox.bottom = bottom;
02653       rclBox.top = top;
02654       ptlStart.x = xstart;
02655       ptlStart.y = ystart;
02656       ptlEnd.x = xend;
02657       ptlEnd.y = yend;
02658     }
02663     EMRARC ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02664     {
02665       ds >> emr >> rclBox >> ptlStart >> ptlEnd;
02666     }
02670     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02671     {
02672       ds << emr << rclBox << ptlStart << ptlEnd;
02673       return true;
02674     }
02678     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02684     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
02685     {
02686       Arc( dc, rclBox.left, rclBox.top, rclBox.right, rclBox.bottom,
02687            ptlStart.x, ptlStart.y, ptlEnd.x, ptlEnd.y );
02688     }
02689 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02690 
02693     void edit ( void ) const
02694     {
02695       printf( "*ARC*\n" );
02696 #if 0
02697       printf( "\trclBox.left    : %ld\n", rclBox.left );
02698       printf( "\trclBox.top     : %ld\n", rclBox.top );
02699       printf( "\trclBox.right   : %ld\n", rclBox.right );
02700       printf( "\trclBox.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBox.bottom );
02701 #else
02702       edit_rectl( "rclBox", rclBox );
02703 #endif
02704       printf( "\tptlStart.x     : %ld\n", ptlStart.x );
02705       printf( "\tptlStart.y     : %ld\n", ptlStart.y );
02706       printf( "\tptlEnd.x       : %ld\n", ptlEnd.x );
02707       printf( "\tptlEnd.y       : %ld\n", ptlEnd.y );
02708     }
02709 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02710   };
02711 
02713 
02716   class EMRARCTO : public METARECORD, ::EMRARCTO {
02717   public:
02729     EMRARCTO ( INT left, INT top, INT right, INT bottom, INT xstart,
02730              INT ystart, INT xend, INT yend )
02731     {
02732       emr.iType = EMR_ARCTO;
02733       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRARCTO );
02734       rclBox.left = left;
02735       rclBox.right = right;
02736       rclBox.bottom = bottom;
02737       rclBox.top = top;
02738       ptlStart.x = xstart;
02739       ptlStart.y = ystart;
02740       ptlEnd.x = xend;
02741       ptlEnd.y = yend;
02742     }
02747     EMRARCTO ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02748     {
02749       ds >> emr >> rclBox >> ptlStart >> ptlEnd;
02750     }
02754     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02755     {
02756       ds << emr << rclBox << ptlStart << ptlEnd;
02757       return true;
02758     }
02762     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02768     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
02769     {
02770       ArcTo( dc, rclBox.left, rclBox.top, rclBox.right, rclBox.bottom,
02771              ptlStart.x, ptlStart.y, ptlEnd.x, ptlEnd.y );
02772     }
02773 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02774 
02777     void edit ( void ) const
02778     {
02779       printf( "*ARCTO*\n" );
02780 #if 0
02781       printf( "\trclBox.left    : %ld\n", rclBox.left );
02782       printf( "\trclBox.top     : %ld\n", rclBox.top );
02783       printf( "\trclBox.right   : %ld\n", rclBox.right );
02784       printf( "\trclBox.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBox.bottom );
02785 #else
02786       edit_rectl( "rclBox", rclBox );
02787 #endif
02788       printf( "\tptlStart.x     : %ld\n", ptlStart.x );
02789       printf( "\tptlStart.y     : %ld\n", ptlStart.y );
02790       printf( "\tptlEnd.x       : %ld\n", ptlEnd.x );
02791       printf( "\tptlEnd.y       : %ld\n", ptlEnd.y );
02792     }
02793 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02794   };
02795 
02797 
02800   class EMRRECTANGLE : public METARECORD, ::EMRRECTANGLE {
02801   public:
02808     EMRRECTANGLE ( INT left, INT top, INT right, INT bottom )
02809     {
02810       emr.iType = EMR_RECTANGLE;
02811       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRRECTANGLE );
02812       rclBox.left = left;
02813       rclBox.right = right;
02814       rclBox.bottom = bottom;
02815       rclBox.top = top;
02816     }
02821     EMRRECTANGLE ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02822     {
02823       ds >> emr >> rclBox;
02824     }
02828     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02829     {
02830       ds << emr << rclBox;
02831       return true;
02832     }
02836     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02842     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
02843     {
02844       Rectangle( dc, rclBox.left, rclBox.top, rclBox.right, rclBox.bottom );
02845     }
02846 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02847 
02850     void edit ( void ) const
02851     {
02852       printf( "*RECTANGLE*\n" );
02853 #if 0
02854       printf( "\trclBox.left    : %ld\n", rclBox.left );
02855       printf( "\trclBox.top     : %ld\n", rclBox.top );
02856       printf( "\trclBox.right   : %ld\n", rclBox.right );
02857       printf( "\trclBox.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBox.bottom );
02858 #else
02859       edit_rectl( "rclBox", rclBox );
02860 #endif
02861     }
02862 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02863   };
02864 
02866

02884       rclBox.bottom = bottom;
02885       rclBox.top = top;
02886     }
02891     EMRELLIPSE ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02892     {
02893       ds >> emr >> rclBox;
02894     }
02898     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02899     {
02900       ds << emr << rclBox;
02901       return true;
02902     }
02906     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02912     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
02913     {
02914       Ellipse( dc, rclBox.left, rclBox.top, rclBox.right, rclBox.bottom );
02915     }

02917 
02920     void edit ( void ) const
02921     {
02922       printf( "*ELLIPSE*\n" );
02923 #if 0
02924       printf( "\trclBox.left    : %ld\n", rclBox.left );
02925       printf( "\trclBox.top     : %ld\n", rclBox.top );
02926       printf( "\trclBox.right   : %ld\n", rclBox.right );
02927       printf( "\trclBox.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBox.bottom );
02928 #else
02929       edit_rectl( "rclBox", rclBox );
02930 #endif
02931     }
02932 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02933   };

02936 
02939   class EMRPOLYLINE : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYLINE {
02940     POINTL* lpoints;
02941   public:
02947     EMRPOLYLINE ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
02948     {
02949       cptl = n;
02950       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
02951       aptl[0].y = 0;
02952 
02953       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINE;
02954       // The (cptl - 1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
02955       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINE ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * ( cptl - 1);
02956 
02957       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
02958 
02959       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
02960         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
02961         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
02962       }
02963 
02964       rclBounds = *bounds;
02965     }
02969     ~EMRPOLYLINE ( )
02970     {
02971       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
02972     }
02977     EMRPOLYLINE ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02978     {
02979       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
02980 
02981       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
02982 
02983       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
02984 
02985       ds >> points;
02986     }
02990     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02991     {
02992       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
02993       return true;
02994     }
02998     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03004     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03005     {
03006       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03007       Polyline( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
03008     }
03009 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03010 
03013     void edit ( void ) const
03014     {
03015       printf( "*POLYLINE*\n" );
03016 #if 0
03017       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
03018       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
03019       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03020       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
03021 #else
03022       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03023 #endif
03024       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
03025       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03026       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
03027         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03028     }
03029 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03030   };
03031 
03033 
03036   class EMRPOLYLINE16 : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYLINE16 {
03037     POINT16* lpoints;
03038   public:
03044     EMRPOLYLINE16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT16* points, INT n )
03045     {
03046       cpts = n;
03047       apts[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03048       apts[0].y = 0;
03049 
03050       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINE16;
03051       // The (cptl - 1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03052       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINE16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * ( cpts - 1);
03053 
03054       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03055 
03056       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03057         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03058         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03059       }
03060 
03061       rclBounds = *bounds;
03062     }
03069     EMRPOLYLINE16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
03070     {
03071       cpts = n;
03072       apts[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03073       apts[0].y = 0;
03074 
03075       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINE16;
03076       // The (cptl - 1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03077       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINE16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * ( cpts - 1);
03078 
03079       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03080 
03081       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03082         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03083         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03084       }
03085 
03086       rclBounds = *bounds;
03087     }
03091     ~EMRPOLYLINE16 ( )
03092     {
03093       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03094     }
03099     EMRPOLYLINE16 ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03100     {
03101       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cpts;
03102 
03103       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03104 
03105       POINT16ARRAY points( lpoints, cpts );
03106 
03107       ds >> points;
03108     }
03112     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03113     {
03114       ds << emr << rclBounds << cpts << POINT16ARRAY( lpoints, cpts );
03115       return true;
03116     }
03120     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03126     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03127     {
03128       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03129       Polyline16( dc, lpoints, cpts );
03130     }
03131 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03132 
03135     void edit ( void ) const
03136     {
03137       printf( "*POLYLINE16*\n" );
03138 #if 0
03139       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
03140       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
03141       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03142       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
03143 #else
03144       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03145 #endif
03146       printf( "\tcpts              : %ld\n", cpts );
03147       printf( "\tapts->\n" );
03148       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cpts; i++ )
03149         printf( "\t\t%d, %d\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03150     }
03151 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03152   };

03158   class EMRPOLYGON : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYGON {
03159     POINTL* lpoints;
03160   public:
03166     EMRPOLYGON ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
03167     {
03168       cptl = n;
03169       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03170       aptl[0].y = 0;
03171 
03172       emr.iType = EMR_POLYGON;
03173       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03174       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYGON ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1);
03175 
03176       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03177 
03178       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03179         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03180         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03181       }
03182 
03183       rclBounds = *bounds;
03184     }
03189     EMRPOLYGON ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03190     {
03191       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
03192 
03193       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03194 
03195       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
03196 
03197       ds >> points;
03198     }
03202     ~EMRPOLYGON ( )
03203     {
03204       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03205     }
03209     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03210     {
03211       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
03212       return true;
03213     }
03217     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03223     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03224     {
03225       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03226       Polygon( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
03227     }
03228 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03229 
03232     void edit ( void ) const
03233     {
03234       printf( "*POLYGON*\n" );
03235 #if 0
03236       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
03237       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
03238       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03239       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
03240 #else
03241       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03242 #endif
03243       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
03244       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03245       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
03246         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03247     }
03248 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03249   };

03255   class EMRPOLYGON16 : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYGON16 {
03256     POINT16* lpoints;
03257   public:

03264     {
03265       cpts = n;
03266       apts[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03267       apts[0].y = 0;
03268 
03269       emr.iType = EMR_POLYGON16;
03270       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out

03274 
03275       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03276         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03277         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03278       }
03279 
03280       rclBounds = *bounds;
03281     }

03289     {

03292       apts[0].y = 0;
03293

03295       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out

03297

03299 
03300       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03301         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03302         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03303       }
03304 
03305       rclBounds = *bounds;
03306     }

03312     {

03314 
03315       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03316

03318

03320     }
03324     ~EMRPOLYGON16 ( )
03325     {
03326       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03327     }
03331     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03332     {
03333       ds << emr << rclBounds << cpts << POINT16ARRAY( lpoints, cpts );
03334       return true;
03335     }
03339     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03345     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03346     {
03347       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03348       Polygon16( dc, lpoints, cpts );
03349     }
03350 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING

03354     void edit ( void ) const
03355     {
03356       printf( "*POLYGON16*\n" );
03357 #if 0
03358       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
03359       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
03360       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03361       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );

03366       printf( "\tapts->\n" );

03369     }
03370 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */

03374 
03377   class EMRPOLYPOLYGON : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON {
03378     DWORD* lcounts;
03379     POINTL* lpoints;
03380   public:
03387     EMRPOLYPOLYGON ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, const INT* counts,
03388                      UINT polygons )
03389     {
03390       nPolys = polygons;
03391       // Count the number of points in points
03392       int n = 0;
03393       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03394         n += counts[i];
03395 
03396       cptl = n;
03397       aPolyCounts[0] = 0;       // Really unused
03398       aptl[0].x = 0;
03399       aptl[0].y = 0;
03400 
03401       emr.iType = EMR_POLYPOLYGON;
03402       // The (#-1)'s below are to account for aPolyCounts[0] and aptl[0], which
03403       // aren't directly written out
03404       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1)
03405         + sizeof( DWORD ) * (nPolys-1);
03406 
03407       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03408 
03409       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03410         lcounts[i] = counts[i];
03411 
03412       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03413 
03414       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03415         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03416         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03417       }
03418 
03419       rclBounds = *bounds;
03420     }
03424     ~EMRPOLYPOLYGON ( )
03425     {
03426       if ( lcounts ) delete[] lcounts;
03427       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03428     }
03433     EMRPOLYPOLYGON ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03434     {
03435       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> nPolys >> cptl;
03436 
03437       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03438 
03439       DWORDARRAY counts( lcounts, nPolys );
03440 
03441       ds >> counts;
03442 
03443       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03444 
03445       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
03446 
03447       ds >> points;
03448     }
03452     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03453     {
03454       ds << emr << rclBounds << nPolys << cptl << DWORDARRAY( lcounts, nPolys )
03455          << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
03456       return true;
03457     }
03461     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03467     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03468     {
03469       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03470       // (but DWORD and INT are not)
03471       INT* counts = new INT[nPolys];
03472       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03473         counts[i] = lcounts[i];
03474 
03475       PolyPolygon( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, counts, nPolys );
03476 
03477       delete[] counts;
03478     }
03479 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03480 
03483     void edit ( void ) const
03484     {
03485       printf( "*POLYPOLYGON*\n" );
03486 #if 0
03487       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
03488       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
03489       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03490       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
03491 #else
03492       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03493 #endif
03494       printf( "\tnPolys            : %ld\n", nPolys );
03495       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
03496       printf( "\taPolyCounts->\n" );
03497       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03498         printf( "\t\t%ld\n", lcounts[i] );
03499       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03500       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
03501         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03502     }
03503 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03504   };
03505 
03507 
03510   class EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 {
03511     DWORD* lcounts;
03512     POINT16* lpoints;
03513   public:
03520     EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points,
03521                        const INT* counts, UINT polygons )
03522     {
03523       nPolys = polygons;
03524       // Count the number of points in points
03525       int n = 0;
03526       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03527         n += counts[i];
03528 
03529       cpts = n;
03530       aPolyCounts[0] = 0;       // Really unused
03531       apts[0].x = 0;
03532       apts[0].y = 0;
03533 
03534       emr.iType = EMR_POLYPOLYGON16;
03535       // The (#-1)'s below are to account for aPolyCounts[0] and aptl[0], which
03536       // aren't directly written out
03537       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * (cpts-1)
03538         + sizeof( DWORD ) * (nPolys-1);
03539 
03540       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03541 
03542       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03543         lcounts[i] = counts[i];
03544 
03545       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03546 
03547       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03548         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03549         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03550       }
03551 
03552       rclBounds = *bounds;
03553     }
03561     EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT16* points,
03562                        const INT* counts, UINT16 polygons )
03563     {
03564       nPolys = polygons;
03565       // Count the number of points in points
03566       int n = 0;
03567       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03568         n += counts[i];
03569 
03570       cpts = n;
03571       aPolyCounts[0] = 0;       // Really unused
03572       apts[0].x = 0;
03573       apts[0].y = 0;
03574 
03575       emr.iType = EMR_POLYPOLYGON16;
03576       // The (#-1)'s below are to account for aPolyCounts[0] and aptl[0], which
03577       // aren't directly written out
03578       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * (cpts-1)
03579         + sizeof( DWORD ) * (nPolys-1);
03580 
03581       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03582 
03583       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03584         lcounts[i] = counts[i];
03585 
03586       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03587 
03588       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03589         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03590         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03591       }
03592 
03593       rclBounds = *bounds;
03594     }
03598     ~EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ( )
03599     {
03600       if ( lcounts ) delete[] lcounts;
03601       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03602     }
03607     EMRPOLYPOLYGON16 ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03608     {
03609       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> nPolys >> cpts;
03610 
03611       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03612 
03613       DWORDARRAY counts( lcounts, nPolys );
03614 
03615       ds >> counts;
03616 
03617       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
03618 
03619       POINT16ARRAY points( lpoints, cpts );
03620 
03621       ds >> points;
03622     }
03626     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03627     {
03628       ds << emr << rclBounds << nPolys << cpts << DWORDARRAY( lcounts, nPolys )
03629          << POINT16ARRAY( lpoints, cpts );
03630       return true;
03631     }
03635     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03641     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03642     {
03643       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03644       // (but DWORD and INT are not)
03645       INT* counts = new INT[nPolys];
03646       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03647         counts[i] = lcounts[i];
03648 
03649       PolyPolygon16( dc, lpoints, counts, nPolys );
03650 
03651       delete[] counts;
03652     }
03653 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03654 
03657     void edit ( void ) const
03658     {
03659       printf( "*POLYPOLYGON16*\n" );
03660 #if 0
03661       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
03662       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
03663       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03664       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
03665 #else
03666       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03667 #endif
03668       printf( "\tnPolys            : %ld\n", nPolys );
03669       printf( "\tcpts              : %ld\n", cpts );
03670       printf( "\taPolyCounts->\n" );
03671       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03672         printf( "\t\t%ld\n", lcounts[i] );
03673       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03674       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cpts; i++ )
03675         printf( "\t\t%d, %d\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03676     }
03677 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03678   };
03679 
03681 
03684   class EMRPOLYBEZIER : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYBEZIER {
03685     POINTL* lpoints;
03686   public:
03692     EMRPOLYBEZIER ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
03693     {
03694       cptl = n;
03695       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03696       aptl[0].y = 0;
03697 
03698       emr.iType = EMR_POLYBEZIER;
03699       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03700       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYBEZIER ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1);
03701 
03702       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03703 
03704       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03705         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03706         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03707       }
03708 
03709       rclBounds = *bounds;
03710     }
03715     EMRPOLYBEZIER ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03716     {
03717       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
03718 
03719       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03720 
03721       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
03722 
03723       ds >> points;
03724     }
03728     ~EMRPOLYBEZIER ( )
03729     {
03730       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03731     }
03735     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03736     {
03737       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
03738       return true;
03739     }
03743     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03749     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
03750     {
03751       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03752       PolyBezier( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
03753     }
03754 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03755 
03758     void edit ( void ) const
03759     {
03760       printf( "*POLYBEZIER*\n" );
03761 #if 0
03762       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
03763       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
03764       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03765       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
03766 #else
03767       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
03768 #endif
03769       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
03770       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03771       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
03772         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03773     }
03774 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03775   };
03776

04123     POINTL* lpoints;

04131     {
04132       cptl = n;
04133       aptl[0].x = 0;
04134       aptl[0].y = 0;
04135 
04136       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINETO;
04137       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
04138       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINETO ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1);
04139 
04140       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
04141 
04142       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
04143         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
04144         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
04145       }
04146 
04147       rclBounds = *bounds;
04148     }
04153     EMRPOLYLINETO ( DATASTREAM& ds )
04154     {
04155       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
04156 
04157       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
04158

04160 
04161       ds >> points;
04162     }
04166     ~EMRPOLYLINETO ( )
04167     {
04168       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
04169     }

04176       return true;
04177     }

04188     {

04190       PolylineTo( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );

04208       printf( "\taptl->\n" );

04338 
04343   class EMREXTTEXTOUTA : public METARECORD, ::EMREXTTEXTOUTA {
04344     PSTR string_a;
04345     int string_size;
04346 
04347     INT* dx_i;
04348   public:
04358     EMREXTTEXTOUTA ( const RECTL* bounds, DWORD graphicsMode, FLOAT xScale,
04359                      FLOAT yScale, const PEMRTEXT text, LPCSTR string,
04360                      const INT* dx )
04361     {
04362       emr.iType = EMR_EXTTEXTOUTA;
04363       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMREXTTEXTOUTA );
04364 
04365       rclBounds = *bounds;
04366 
04367       iGraphicsMode = graphicsMode;
04368       exScale = xScale;
04369       eyScale = yScale;
04370 
04371       emrtext = *text;
04372 
04373       string_size = ROUND_TO_LONG( emrtext.nChars );
04374 
04375       string_a = new CHAR[ string_size ];
04376 
04377       memset( string_a, 0, sizeof(CHAR) * string_size );
04378 
04379       for ( unsigned int i=0; i<emrtext.nChars; i++ )
04380         string_a[i] = *string++;
04381 
04382       emrtext.offString = emr.nSize;
04383       emr.nSize += string_size * sizeof(CHAR);
04384 
04385       if ( dx ) {
04386 
04387         dx_i = new INT[ emrtext.nChars ];
04388 
04389         for ( unsigned int i=0; i<emrtext.nChars; i++ )
04390           dx_i[i] = *dx++;
04391 
04392         emrtext.offDx = emr.nSize;
04393         emr.nSize += emrtext.nChars * sizeof(INT);
04394       }
04395       else {
04396         emrtext.offDx = 0;
04397         dx_i = 0;
04398       }
04399     }
04404     EMREXTTEXTOUTA ( DATASTREAM& ds )
04405     {
04406       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> iGraphicsMode >> exScale >> eyScale >> emrtext;
04407 
04408       if ( emrtext.offString != 0 ) {
04409         string_size = ROUND_TO_LONG( emrtext.nChars );
04410 
04411         string_a = new CHAR[ string_size ];
04412 
04413         memset( string_a, 0, sizeof(CHAR) * string_size );
04414 
04415         CHARSTR string( string_a, string_size );
04416 
04417         ds >> string;
04418       }
04419       else
04420         string_a = 0;
04421 
04422       if ( emrtext.offDx ) {
04423         dx_i = new INT[ emrtext.nChars ];
04424 
04425         INTARRAY dx_is( dx_i, emrtext.nChars );
04426 
04427         ds >> dx_is;
04428       }
04429       else
04430         dx_i = 0;
04431     }
04436     ~EMREXTTEXTOUTA ( )
04437     {
04438       if ( string_a ) delete[] string_a;
04439       if ( dx_i ) delete[] dx_i;
04440     }
04444     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
04445     {
04446       ds << emr << rclBounds << iGraphicsMode << exScale << eyScale
04447          << emrtext << CHARSTR( string_a, string_size );
04448       if ( dx_i )
04449         ds << INTARRAY( dx_i, emrtext.nChars );
04450       return true;
04451     }
04455     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
04461     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
04462     {
04463       RECT rect;
04464       rect.left = emrtext.rcl.left;
04465       rect.top = emrtext.rcl.top;
04466       rect.right = emrtext.rcl.right;
04467       rect.bottom = emrtext.rcl.bottom;
04468 
04469       ExtTextOutA( dc, emrtext.ptlReference.x, emrtext.ptlReference.y,
04470                    emrtext.fOptions, &rect, string_a, emrtext.nChars,
04471                    dx_i );
04472     }
04473 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
04474 
04477     void edit ( void ) const
04478     {
04479       printf( "*EXTTEXTOUTA*\n" );
04480 #if 0
04481       printf( "\trclBounds: (%ld,%ld)-(%ld,%ld)\n", rclBounds.left, rclBounds.top,
04482               rclBounds.right, rclBounds.bottom );
04483 #else
04484       edit_rectl( "rclBounds", rclBounds );
04485 #endif
04486       printf( "\tiGraphicsMode:" );
04487       switch ( iGraphicsMode ) {
04488       case GM_COMPATIBLE: printf( "GM_COMPATIBLE\n" ); break;
04489       case GM_ADVANCED: printf( "GM_ADVANCED\n" ); break;
04490       default: printf( "unknown(%ld)\n", iGraphicsMode );
04491       }
04492       printf( "\texScale: %f\n", exScale );
04493       printf( "\teyScale: %f\n", eyScale );
04494       printf( "\tptlReference: (%ld,%ld)\n", emrtext.ptlReference.x,
04495               emrtext.ptlReference.y );
04496       printf( "\tnChars: %ld\n", emrtext.nChars );
04497       printf( "\toffString: %ld\n", emrtext.offString );
04498       printf( "\tfOptions: " );
04499       if ( emrtext.fOptions & ETO_GRAYED )
04500         printf( "ETO_GRAYED | " );
04501       if ( emrtext.fOptions & ETO_OPAQUE )
04502         printf( "ETO_OPAQUE | " );
04503       if ( emrtext.fOptions & ETO_CLIPPED )
04504         printf( "ETO_CLIPPED | " );
04505       if ( emrtext.fOptions & ETO_GLYPH_INDEX )
04506         printf( "ETO_GLYPH_INDEX | " );
04507       if ( emrtext.fOptions & ETO_RTLREADING )
04508         printf( "ETO_RTLREADING | " );
04509       if ( emrtext.fOptions & ETO_IGNORELANGUAGE )
04510         printf( "ETO_IGNORELANGUAGE" );
04511       printf( "\n" );
04512 #if 0
04513       printf( "\trcl: (%ld,%ld)-(%ld,%ld)\n", emrtext.rcl.left, emrtext.rcl.top,
04514               emrtext.rcl.right, emrtext.rcl.bottom );
04515 #else
04516       edit_rectl( "rcl", emrtext.rcl );
04517 #endif
04518       printf( "\toffDx: %ld\n", emrtext.offDx );
04519 
04520       printf( "\tString:\n\t\t%s\n",  string_a );
04521 
04522       if ( emrtext.offDx != 0 ) {
04523         printf( "\tOffsets:\n\t\t" );
04524         for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < emrtext.nChars; i++ )
04525           printf( "%d ", dx_i[i] );
04526         printf( "\n" );
04527       }
04528     }
04529 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
04530   };
04531 
04533 
04536   class EMRSETPIXELV : public METARECORD, ::EMRSETPIXELV {
04537   public:
04543     EMRSETPIXELV ( INT x, INT y, COLORREF color )
04544     {
04545       emr.iType = EMR_SETPIXELV;
04546       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRSETPIXELV );
04547       ptlPixel.x = x;
04548       ptlPixel.y = y;
04549       crColor = color;
04550     }
04555     EMRSETPIXELV ( DATASTREAM& ds )
04556     {
04557       ds >> emr >> ptlPixel >> crColor;
04558     }
04562     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
04563     {
04564       ds << emr << ptlPixel << crColor;
04565       return true;
04566     }
04570     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
04576     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* /*source*/, HDC dc ) const
04577     {
04578       SetPixel( dc, ptlPixel.x, ptlPixel.y, crColor );
04579     }
04580 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
04581 
04584     void edit ( void ) const
04585     {
04586       printf( "*SETPIXELV*\n" );
04587       printf( "\tptlPixel: %ld, %ld\n", ptlPixel.x, ptlPixel.y );
04588       printf( "\tcrColor: 0x%lx\n", crColor );
04589     }
04590 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
04591   };
04592 
04593   class PEN;

04595   class BRUSH;
04596   class FONT;
04597   class PALETTE;
